vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-48746
vulnerability.description: vLLM starlette/ASGI authentication bypass of the OpenAI API AuthenticationMiddleware (GHSA-94f4-hr76-p5j6, CWE-444). Affects vLLM 0.3.0 through 0.22.0; fixed in 0.22.0 (vllm PR #43426).

statements.impact_statement: The vulnerability lets an unauthenticated network caller bypass the vLLM OpenAI-API key check (VLLM_API_KEY/--api-key). In this disaggregated-inference deployment that attack surface is not reachable by an adversary: pods run unprivileged with NO hostNetwork, NO Service or container port is published to 0.0.0.0/0, and the frontend is accessed only via a 127.0.0.1 kubectl port-forward for benchmarking. The deployment does not rely on VLLM_API_KEY as a security boundary, so bypassing it grants access to no otherwise-protected resource. NOTE: this justification is conditional on the network posture above. If the vLLM/frontend API is ever exposed beyond the local node (LoadBalancer, NodePort, Ingress, or an intra-cluster Service relied upon for isolation), this VEX statement MUST be revisited and the image upgraded to a vLLM build that includes PR #43426 (i.e. NVIDIA's next -efa tag rebased on a patched vLLM, or a backport).
